entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop grown in an intercropping system with pearl millet

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum americanum, a cereal crop used in intercropping

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same field

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period during which the microclimate and productivity were monitored

entities.name: ICRISAT HYDERABAD INDIA
entities.description: The research site where the intercropping study was conducted

entities.name: MICROCLIMATE
entities.description: The local atmospheric conditions within the crop canopy

entities.name: WIND SPEED
entities.description: A microclimate variable reduced by the height of the pearl millet canopy

entities.name: LEAF WETNESS DURATION
entities.description: The period during which leaf surfaces remain wet, increased by intercropping

entities.name: DEFOLIATION
entities.description: The loss of leaves from the plant, potentially influenced by leaf wetness

entities.name: THERMAL TIME
entities.description: A calculation used to determine the development rate of groundnuts

entities.name: SATURATION DEFICIT
entities.description: A microclimate factor with a small effect on crop growth

entities.name: CROP GROWTH
entities.description: The overall development and growth process of the crops

entities.name: RADIATION USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which groundnuts utilize solar radiation, increased by intercropping

entities.name: YIELD LOSS
entities.description: The reduction in grain yield caused by foliar diseases

entities.name: FOLIAR DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases affecting the leaves of groundnuts

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: FUNGICIDE
entities.description: A chemical treatment used to control foliar diseases

entities.name: LAND EQUIVALENT RATIO
entities.description: A metric used to evaluate the productivity of the intercrop system

relationships.description: Intercropping involves the cultivation of groundnut
Intercropping involves the cultivation of pearl millet
Intercropping alters the microclimate of the crops
The canopy height of pearl millet reduces wind speed
Intercropping increases the leaf wetness duration of groundnuts
Leaf wetness duration has little effect on defoliation
Thermal time is used to measure the development of groundnuts
Saturation deficit has a small effect on crop growth
Intercropping increases the radiation use efficiency of groundnuts
Foliar diseases cause yield loss in groundnuts
Intercropping has no significant effect on the severity of foliar diseases
Fungicide application is used to protect grain yield from diseases
Land equivalent ratio is used to measure the productivity of the intercrop
The microclimate was monitored during the rainy season
The intercropping study was conducted at ICRISAT Hyderabad India
